security.title: Security and Privacy
security.subtitle: Your files are handled with care. We do not store, share, or analyze the content of any file you convert through FlipMyFiles.

feature1.title: Encrypted Transfers
feature1.description: All uploads and downloads are transmitted over HTTPS with TLS encryption, protecting your files during transfer.

feature2.title: No File Storage
feature2.description: Server-side conversions process files in memory only. Files are not saved to disk and are discarded after conversion.

feature3.title: Browser-Based Processing
feature3.description: Video and audio conversions run entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Those files never leave your device.

feature4.title: No Account Required
feature4.description: Use the full conversion service without creating an account, providing personal information, or signing up for anything.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about FlipMyFiles and how the service works.
faq.q1: What file formats does FlipMyFiles support?
faq.a1: FlipMyFiles supports conversion across images (JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, BMP, TIFF, AVIF, SVG, ICO, HEIC), video (MP4, AVI, MOV, MKV, WebM, FLV, WMV), audio (MP3, WAV, OGG, FLAC, AAC, WMA, M4A), documents (DOCX, PDF, TXT, CSV, XLSX), and archives (ZIP, TAR, GZ). Visit the Tools page for a complete list of all available conversion pairs.
faq.q2: Is FlipMyFiles really free to use?
faq.a2: Yes. FlipMyFiles is free to use with no hidden fees, no premium tiers, and no watermarks on converted files. The service is supported by advertising, which allows us to keep it accessible to everyone.
faq.q3: Do I need to create an account?
faq.a3: No. FlipMyFiles does not require registration, an email address, or any personal information. You can start converting files immediately without signing up.
faq.q4: What happens to my files after conversion?
faq.a4: Server-side conversions (images, documents, archives) are processed in memory and discarded immediately after the result is returned to your browser. Video and audio files are converted entirely within your browser and are never uploaded to our servers.
faq.q5: What is the maximum file size I can upload?
faq.a5: The current file size limit is 250 MB per file. This applies to all file types. For video and audio files, conversion speed depends on your device since processing happens in the browser.
faq.q6: Why do some conversions happen in the browser?
faq.a6: Video and audio conversions use FFmpeg compiled to WebAssembly, which runs directly in your browser. This means those files never leave your device, providing an extra layer of privacy. Image and document conversions are handled server-side for faster processing.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: A free, browser-based file conversion tool built for speed, privacy, and simplicity. No account. No software. No limits.
about.intro: FlipMyFiles is a browser-based file conversion tool designed to help users convert images, videos, audio files, and documents between common formats. The service is free to use and does not require an account or registration.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: We created FlipMyFiles with a straightforward goal: provide a reliable file conversion tool that respects user privacy and works directly in the browser. Many existing conversion tools require software downloads, account creation, or paid subscriptions for basic features. FlipMyFiles removes those barriers by offering conversion tools accessible to anyone with a web browser.

why.title: Why FlipMyFiles Was Built
why.p1: File format compatibility remains a common challenge. Whether you need to convert a photo taken on your phone to a format your editing software supports, extract audio from a video, or turn a spreadsheet into a CSV for data import, these tasks come up regularly for both personal and professional use.
why.p2: FlipMyFiles was built to address these needs in a simple, accessible way. Rather than installing desktop software or navigating complex settings, users can upload a file, select the output format, and download the result within seconds.

whatWeSupport.title: What We Support
whatWeSupport.intro: FlipMyFiles supports conversion across several file categories:
whatWeSupport.images: Images: JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, BMP, TIFF, AVIF, SVG, ICO, and HEIC
whatWeSupport.video: Video: MP4, AVI, MOV, MKV, WebM, FLV, and WMV
whatWeSupport.audio: Audio: MP3, WAV, OGG, FLAC, AAC, WMA, and M4A
whatWeSupport.documents: Documents: DOCX, PDF, TXT, CSV, and XLSX
whatWeSupport.processing: Video and audio conversions are processed entirely in your browser using WebAssembly technology, meaning those files never leave your device. Image and document conversions are processed on the server and the data is discarded immediately after the conversion completes.

privacy.title: Privacy and File Handling
privacy.intro: We take file handling seriously. Here is how the service works:
privacy.point1: Files processed server-side are handled in memory and are not written to permanent storage
privacy.point2: Video and audio files are converted entirely in your browser and never uploaded to our servers
privacy.point3: No user accounts or personal information are collected
privacy.point4: All file transfers are encrypted using HTTPS
privacy.point5: We do not analyze, index, or retain the content of uploaded files
privacy.readMore: For more details, please review our

limitations.title: Limitations
limitations.intro: We believe in being transparent about what the service can and cannot do:
limitations.point1: The maximum upload size is 250 MB per file
limitations.point2: Some format combinations may produce varying results depending on the source file's encoding or complexity
limitations.point3: Video and audio conversion speed depends on your device's processing power since those conversions run in the browser
limitations.point4: Document conversions (such as DOCX to PDF) handle text content well but may not preserve complex formatting, embedded objects, or custom fonts
limitations.point5: The service may experience temporary downtime during maintenance or updates
limitations.contact: We continuously work to improve conversion quality and add support for additional formats. If you encounter an issue or would like to request a new format, feel free to reach out through our

technology.title: Technology
technology.text: FlipMyFiles uses a combination of server-side and client-side processing to handle conversions. Image conversions use the Sharp library, a high-performance image processing tool. Video and audio conversions use FFmpeg compiled to WebAssembly, allowing those conversions to happen directly in the browser without sending data to a server. Document conversions rely on specialized libraries for each format type.
technology.cardText: FlipMyFiles is built with Next.js, TypeScript, and React on the frontend, Sharp for high-performance server-side image processing, and FFmpeg compiled to WebAssembly for in-browser video and audio conversion. Your files are handled by battle-tested, widely-used open source libraries.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Have a question, found a bug, or want to suggest a new format? We'd love to hear from you.

howItWorksPage.title: Convert Files in Three Steps
howItWorksPage.subtitle: FlipMyFiles converts your files directly in the browser or on our servers — no downloads, no account, no waiting. Here is exactly how the process works.

steps.s1desc: Drag and drop your file onto the upload area, or click to browse your device. You can upload images, videos, audio files, documents, and archives up to 250 MB.
steps.s2desc: Select the format you want to convert to from the dropdown. Available options are shown based on your input file type. Some converters offer optional quality or compression settings.
steps.s3desc: Once conversion is complete, your file is ready to download immediately. Click the download button and the converted file saves directly to your device.

processing.title: How Files Are Processed
processing.subtitle: Different file types use different conversion methods to ensure the best results and privacy.

browser.title: Browser-Based (Video & Audio)
browser.desc: Video and audio conversions run entirely in your browser using FFmpeg compiled to WebAssembly (WASM). Your files are never uploaded to our servers — the entire conversion happens locally on your device.
browser.p1: Files stay on your device at all times
browser.p2: Works offline once the converter is loaded
browser.p3: Conversion speed depends on your device hardware
browser.p4: No file size restrictions from server bandwidth

server.title: Server-Side (Images & Documents)
server.desc: Image and document conversions are processed on our servers using the Sharp library for images and specialized libraries for documents. Files are handled in memory and discarded immediately after the converted file is delivered.
server.p1: Processed in memory — never written to disk
server.p4: No user data or file content is retained

faqs.title: Common Questions
faqs.q1: Do I need to create an account?
faqs.a1: No. FlipMyFiles does not require registration or login. You can start converting files immediately without creating an account.
faqs.q2: What is the maximum file size?
faqs.a2: The maximum upload size is 250 MB per file. For video and audio files processed in-browser, this limit is governed by your device's available memory.
faqs.a3: Image and document conversions are typically completed in under a few seconds. Video and audio conversions run in your browser and depend on file size and your device's processing power — larger files may take longer.
faqs.q4: Are my files stored after conversion?
faqs.a4: No. Files processed server-side are handled entirely in memory and discarded as soon as you download the result. Video and audio files never leave your device at all.
faqs.q5: What happens if a conversion fails?
faqs.a5: If a conversion fails, an error message will be displayed. Common causes include unsupported source encoding, corrupted files, or files that exceed the size limit. You can try again or contact us if the issue persists.
